description:
Advances: mutagen.mutators

Deliverable:
- `lib/mutagen_ex/mutators.ex` (module `MutagenEx.Mutators`) â€” registry that
  enumerates the 10 mutators below.
- Per-mutator modules under `lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/`:
  `arith.ex`, `compare.ex`, `boolean.ex`, `literal.ex`, `with_swap.ex`,
  `case_drop.ex`, `pipeline.ex`, `result_tuple.ex`, `else_removal.ex`,
  `guard_drop.ex`. Each module defines `match?/1`, `mutate/1`, `validate/1`.
- Content-addressed site ID format: `{relative_file}:{ast_hash}:{mutator_name}`
  where `ast_hash` is `:erlang.phash2/2` of the normalized AST node (strip
  `:line`, `:column`, `:end_line`, `:end_column` from metadata before
  hashing). Per [mutagen.decision.content_addressed_ids](.spec/decisions/content_addressed_ids.md).
- Per-mutator unit tests at `test/mutagen_ex/mutators/<mutator>_test.exs`
  (one per mutator).
- `test/mutagen_ex/mutators_test.exs` for the registry.
- `test/mutagen_ex/mutators_property_test.exs` covering symmetric-swap
  invariants and round-trip `Macro.to_string/1` â†’ `Code.string_to_quoted/2`.
- `test/mutagen_ex/mutators/id_stability_test.exs` â€” golden test asserting
  byte-identical IDs across 10 invocations on the same source.

Verification:
- Tests to pass: `mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutators_test.exs`,
  `mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutators_property_test.exs`,
  `mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutators/`.
- Covers spec-verification stubs `mutagen.mutators.v1`, `.v2`, `.v3`, `.v4`.
- Behaviors to demonstrate: scenarios `mutagen.mutators.s1` through `s6`.

Out of Scope (files):
- `lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator.ex` (S4b â€” consumes the catalog)
- `lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex` (S5)
- Anything outside the mutators/ subtree

Allowed touches (this stage may modify):
- `lib/mutagen_ex/mutators.ex`
- `lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/*.ex`
- `test/mutagen_ex/mutators_test.exs`
- `test/mutagen_ex/mutators_property_test.exs`
- `test/mutagen_ex/mutators/*.exs`

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do not run any mutated code. The catalog is pure AST manipulation; runtime
  classification (`:killed`, `:survived`, `:timeout`, `:compile_error`, `:error`)
  is the runner's job in S5.
- Do not add third-party deps. `StreamData` is the only acceptable test-only
  dep; check whether it's already in `mix.exs` before adding.
- Do not introduce mutator config (catalog is closed in v1 per
  [mutagen.decision.validate_predicates](.spec/decisions/validate_predicates.md)).

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly with `--warnings-as-errors`
- [ ] All mutator unit tests + property tests + ID stability test pass
- [ ] Spec verification passes (`mutagen.mutators.v1`-`.v4`)
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: fa1552c1-29af-4847-9fbc-5065c95d3be9
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/fa1552c1-29af-4847-9fbc-5065c95d3be9.jsonl

Commit: 22a378445f77128cb2cdbd541de5dc5c31bc268d (on branch mutagen-wrd.5/mutator-catalog, rebased onto epic HEAD 0c885cf)

Files touched:
- .spec/specs/mutators.spec.md (verification stubs flipped, see below)
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutators.ex (registry + behaviour + site_id/3 + AST normalization)
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/{arith,boolean,case_drop,compare,else_removal,guard_drop,literal,pipeline,result_tuple,with_swap}.ex (10 mutators)
- test/mutagen_ex/mutators_test.exs (registry + cross-cutting requirements)
- test/mutagen_ex/mutators_property_test.exs (r5 symmetric-swap involution + r6 Macro.to_string round-trip)
- test/mutagen_ex/mutators/{arith,boolean,case_drop,compare,else_removal,guard_drop,id_stability,literal,pipeline,result_tuple,with_swap}_test.exs (per-mutator unit tests + golden ID stability)

Tests added (modules):
- MutagenEx.MutatorsTest (15 tests)
- MutagenEx.MutatorsPropertyTest (8 tests)
- MutagenEx.Mutators.{Arith,Boolean,CaseDrop,Compare,ElseRemoval,GuardDrop,Literal,Pipeline,ResultTuple,WithSwap}Test (~10 tests each = 92 tests)
- MutagenEx.Mutators.IdStabilityTest (4 tests)
Total: 115 new tests covering r1-r7. Full suite (`mix test`): 1 doctest + 202 tests, 0 failures.

Verification stubs flipped (execute: false -> execute: true):
- mutagen.mutators.v1 (covers r1, r2) â€” `mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutators_test.exs` -> 15/15 pass
- mutagen.mutators.v2 (covers r3, r4) â€” `mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutators/id_stability_test.exs` -> 4/4 pass
- mutagen.mutators.v3 (covers r5, r6) â€” `mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutators_property_test.exs` -> 8/8 pass
- mutagen.mutators.v4 (covers r2) â€” `mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutators/ --only validate` -> 24/24 pass (68 excluded by tag)

Audit-expanded scope: editing `.spec/specs/mutators.spec.md` was required by the merge gate ("Spec verification passes (mutagen.mutators.v1-.v4)"), since stubs ship with `execute: false` from /distill. Per orchestrator note in this stage's worker prompt (precedent: mutagen-wrd.1 and mutagen-wrd.4), flipping the four stubs to `execute: true` is recognized as planner oversight and authorized for the implementer. No other spec content changed.

Deferred must requirements: none. All seven r1-r7 requirements have at least one direct test:
- r1 (callbacks): MutagenEx.MutatorsTest "every catalog module exports match?/1, mutate/1, validate/1, name/0" â€” would fail if any mutator dropped a callback.
- r2 (skip vocabulary): MutagenEx.MutatorsTest "the v1 skip vocabulary covers the three reasons named in the spec" + per-mutator validate/1 tests â€” would fail if any validate/1 returned an out-of-vocab reason or wrong shape.
- r3 (ID format): MutagenEx.MutatorsTest "produces `relative_file:hash:mutator_name`" and "ID hash uses :erlang.phash2 of the normalised AST" â€” would fail if site_id/3 emitted a different shape or used unnormalized AST.
- r4 (stability across mix format): MutagenEx.Mutators.IdStabilityTest "ID set is invariant under `mix format`-style reformatting" â€” would fail if line/column metadata leaked into the hash.
- r5 (symmetric-swap involution): MutagenEx.MutatorsPropertyTest "mutate âˆ˜ mutate == identity" suite â€” would fail if any symmetric mutator drifted.
- r6 (Macro.to_string round-trip): MutagenEx.MutatorsPropertyTest "Macro.to_string round-trip succeeds for every :ok-validated swap" â€” would fail if a validated swap produced un-reparseable source.
- r7 (closed catalog): MutagenEx.MutatorsTest "all/0 returns exactly the ten v1 mutators in the spec's canonical order" + "names/0 returns one snake_case atom per catalog entry" â€” would fail if a mutator was added without updating both the registry and the spec.

Summary: Implemented the ten v1 mutators per the `mutagen.mutators` spec, each as a module under `MutagenEx.Mutators.*` exposing the four-callback contract (`name/0`, `match?/1`, `mutate/1`, `validate/1`). The shared `MutagenEx.Mutators` module is the closed registry and also owns `site_id/3` + AST normalization (strips :line/:column/:end_line/:end_column) so site IDs are stable across `mix format` per `mutagen.decision.content_addressed_ids`. `WithSwap.validate/1` surfaces `:bound_var_used_before_binding` (scenario s2) via a free-variable / pattern-bound-variable disjoint check; `Pipeline.validate/1` surfaces `:no_op_shadowed` when two adjacent pipe segments are textually identical after normalization. No third-party deps added; property tests use a deterministic seeded sample space rather than StreamData to keep this stage zero-dep â€” `mix.exs` was not touched.

title: S4a: Mutator catalog + validate predicates
